description: The slippery protective fluid made by moist inner surfaces of the body is 'nuwesu'. It is familiar in the nose and throat but occurs elsewhere too. A sentence can add its colour or thickness and what caused it; the noun simply identifies the fluid.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and release into high-back rounded 'u'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move into stressed middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Bring the tongue blade close to the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's' and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.

semantic_domains.physical: a protective bodily fluid made by moist inner surfaces
